skill.name: postmortem-writing
skill.description: Helps teams write blameless incident postmortems with timelines, root cause analysis, lessons learned, and clear action items.
skill.summary: Create structured, blameless postmortems that turn incidents into practical improvements.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives caused by Markdown fences, inline code examples, incident timestamps, and documentation links. No executable scripts, active network calls, secret access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in Markdown code fences or inline code examples used to show postmortem templates. They do not execute commands, interpolate user input, or define runnable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are visible Markdown formatting or inline example text. There is no Ruby file, shell script, executable block, or instruction to run these snippets.

low_findings.title: Incident Example Text Misclassified as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography, at scheduling, and system reconnaissance findings are ordinary postmortem language, timestamps, and question prompts. They do not invoke cryptographic APIs, schedule jobs, or inspect a host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose inside a writing guide. No code path, command invocation, or operational instruction matches the static labels semantically.

low_findings.title: External Reference Links Are Passive Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URL findings are resource links to postmortem guidance, not automatic network requests. Users may choose to open them, but the skill does not fetch data or transmit information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a Resources section as Markdown links. There is no HTTP client code, callback URL, or exfiltration target.

low_findings.title: Metrics Example Misclassified as Network Library Use
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The Python HTTP library finding at line 241 is an incident evidence example about connection counts. It contains no import, client call, or network operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain Markdown text in a 5 Whys example. No Python syntax or HTTP library usage is present in the reviewed context.

content.value_statement: Incident reviews often lose value when timelines are vague or action items are weak. This skill helps teams create clear, blameless postmortems with root causes, lessons, and follow-up work.
actual_capabilities: Drafts standard postmortem documents with executive summaries, timelines, impact, detection, response, and lessons learned.
Guides 5 Whys analysis for identifying primary, secondary, and systemic root causes.
Creates quick postmortems for minor incidents with concise timelines and fixes.
Supports blameless meeting facilitation with agenda structure and discussion prompts.
Turns incident lessons into owned action items with priority, due date, and tracking fields.
limitations: Requires accurate incident facts, logs, timestamps, and impact data from the user.
Does not investigate systems, query monitoring tools, or validate technical evidence by itself.
Does not replace legal, compliance, or customer communication review for sensitive incidents.
Cannot guarantee action item completion without external tracking and ownership.

use_cases.title: Draft a Major Incident Review
use_cases.description: Create a complete postmortem after a customer-facing outage, including impact, timeline, causes, and prevention actions.

use_cases.title: Facilitate a Blameless Review Meeting
use_cases.description: Prepare a focused meeting agenda that encourages learning, avoids blame, and produces clear follow-up work.

use_cases.title: Analyze Repeated Minor Incidents
use_cases.description: Convert small incident notes into quick postmortems that reveal recurring patterns and practical improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Postmortem
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the postmortem-writing skill to draft a blameless postmortem. Incident facts: [paste summary, timeline, impact, fix, and open questions].

prompt_templates.title: Run 5 Whys Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the postmortem-writing skill to perform a 5 Whys analysis for this incident. Separate proximate causes, contributing factors, and systemic fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Review Meeting Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the postmortem-writing skill to create a 60-minute review agenda, facilitation notes, and decision points for this incident: [paste incident context].

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the postmortem-writing skill to review this draft for blame language, missing evidence, weak action items, and unclear ownership: [paste draft].

output_examples.input: A payment outage lasted 47 minutes after a deployment changed database connection behavior.
output_examples.output: A structured postmortem with an executive summary, UTC timeline, customer impact, root cause analysis, detection gaps, response notes, lessons learned, and owned action items.

output_examples.input: A cache flush caused elevated API latency for 12 minutes.
output_examples.output: A quick postmortem that records what happened, the short timeline, root cause, immediate fix, long-term fix, and one clear lesson.

output_examples.input: A team needs to hold a review meeting after a SEV2 incident.
output_examples.output: A 60-minute facilitation plan with opening norms, timeline review, analysis discussion, action item planning, and closing confirmation.

best_practices: Provide exact timestamps, affected systems, customer impact, and evidence before asking for a final draft.
Review generated language for blame and replace personal fault with system conditions.
Convert every major lesson into an owned, dated, trackable action item.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to assign personal blame or justify punishment after an incident.
Writing action items that are broad, ownerless, or impossible to verify.
Publishing a postmortem before technical owners confirm the timeline and evidence.

faq.question: Can this skill investigate the incident for me?
faq.answer: No. It organizes and improves the information you provide, but it does not query logs, metrics, or production systems.

faq.question: Does it support minor incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a quick postmortem format for small incidents and near misses.

faq.question: Can it help remove blame from a draft?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify blame language and suggest system-focused wording.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best output?
faq.answer: Use exact timelines, impact numbers, mitigation steps, known causes, supporting evidence, and unresolved questions.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security incidents?
faq.answer: It can structure the review, but sensitive findings should also receive security, legal, and compliance review.

faq.question: Can it create action items?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft prioritized action items with owners, due dates, ticket references, and expected prevention value.
